channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: JDK 24: The new features in Java 24
item.description: Java Development Kit (JDK) 23 having arrived September 17, work already has begun on JDK 24, with three features so far proposed for the release: warnings to prepare developers for future restrictions on the use of JNI (Java Native Interface) and a late barrier expansion for the G1 garbage collector
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: iPhone stuck in SOS mode? Here's how to get out of it
item.description: There are ongoing reports of a widespread outage impacting multiple cellphone and internet providers Monday morning. According to Downdetector.com, AT&T, Verizon and T-Mobile users have all experienced issues.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, NewsNation

item.title: New leaks suggest AMD’s RDNA 4-based GPUs will launch early 2025
item.description: AMD’s next-gen lineup of RDNA 4-based graphics cards are now expected to launch in 2025, according to sources close to YouTube channel Moore’s Law Is Dead.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: 2024 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Can digital conversations help disaster-proof your business?
item.description: “The only thing certain in life is uncertainty.” This quote has been attributed to everyone from Pliny the Elder to John F. Kennedy, which means it’s probably been around longer than recorded history and reflects a fundamental truth about how we live. It’s also a fundamental truth about how we work.
item.dc:creator: Ruth Zive, Fast Company

item.title: The worst programmer I know
item.description: If all code is legacy code, then we are all maintainers. I carry an image in my head of a vast sea of silent, diligent, dedicated coders who silently maintain much of the code that makes the world go round. I carry this image because I am one of those coders.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Verizon customers report major outage on mobile network
item.description: Tens of thousands of Verizon users reported outages for the wireless carrier’s services across the U.S. on Monday, with Chicago and Indianapolis among the hardest-hit cities and some iPhone users stuck in “SOS” mode. According to tracking website Downdetector.com, the outage began at around 9:30 a.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Arkansas sues YouTube over claims that the site is fueling a mental health crisis
item.description: Arkansas has sued YouTube and its parent company Alphabet, saying the video-sharing platform is deliberately addictive and fueling a mental health crisis among youth.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Demillo, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: DirecTV and Dish Agree to Merge, Creating Largest Pay-TV Provider in US
item.description: DirecTV and Dish agree to merge, creating the largest pay-TV provider in the US with 18 million subscribers. The deal is contingent upon bondholders agreeing to take a haircut on debt.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Arm, Intel, and the vengeance of Newton
item.description: Arm’s attempt to purchase part of Intel’s computer chip business may have been declined, but it once again demonstrates the important role played in the evolution of today’s tech by Apple and its handheld Newton.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Why the internet loves making fun of JD Vance
item.description: When it comes to memes on the internet, Ohio Senator JD Vance is the gift that keeps on giving. Whatever Vance’s prep strategy for the upcoming vice presidential debate, we know at least one thing: We’re likely to get a few memes out of the event.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Verizon, AT&T, T-Mobile service outages reported by users
item.description: People in multiple cities posted about issues they were having, with many saying their phones were in "SOS" mode.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: San Francisco rolls out AI-powered cameras to combat crime
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson discusses San Francisco deploying three advanced mobile surveillance cameras to deter crime in high-risk areas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Newsom vetoes bill for stricter AI regulations
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom (D) on Sunday vetoed a landmark artificial intelligence (AI) bill that would have created new safety rules for the emerging tech, handing much of Silicon Valley a major win.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: California governor vetoes bill to create first-in-nation AI safety measures
item.description: The decision is a blow to efforts attempting to rein in the industry that is rapidly evolving with little oversight.
item.dc:creator: TRÂN NGUYỄN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The metaverse never happened, but Mark Zuckerberg’s got some funky glasses
item.description: This story originally appeared in The Technology Letter and is republished here with permission. Oh, the Metaverse! Remember that bit of nonsense, from three years ago? Deep in the pandemic lockdowns, it seemed we all would be living in a digital cave for the rest of eternity.
item.dc:creator: Tiernan Ray, Fast Company

item.title: OpenAI expects about $5 billion in losses this year, report says
item.description: OpenAI expects roughly $5 billion in losses this year, according to reports from The New York Times and CNBC. The company, which owns ChatGPT, had $3.7 billion in revenue this year and expects to increase revenue to over $11 billion next year, The Times initially reported.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, The Hill

item.title: Elon Musk embraces role as pro-Trump 'megaphone'
item.description: Elon Musk has gone from a tech entrepreneur largely avoidant of politics to what strategists call a “megaphone” for right-wing views as an online surrogate for former President Trump in the White House race.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: AI bad actors 'not very Christian'
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lionsgate's bold move into AI is about to change filmmaking forever
item.description: Lionsgate's revolutionary move to partner with tech company Runway to integrate AI into its production process is going to take the film industry by storm.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How much are companies willing to spend to get workers back to the office?
item.description: With more and more companies wanting to bring employees back to the office, I pointed out last week the ill-kept secret that there’s a widespread aversion to open office floor plans — or activity-based workplaces, as they have often evolved into today — and that it partially explains why many employ
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Demystifying AI for the public sector
item.description: Government agencies are understandably hesitant about artificial intelligence. But with the right partners, AI can revolutionize government services. The post Demystifying AI for the public sector first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Amazon's $4 billion partnership with AI startup Anthropic gets UK competition clearance
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Britain's competition watchdog said Friday that it's clearing Amazon's partnership with artificial intelligence company Anthropic because the $4 billion deal didn't qualify for further scrutiny.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Small size, big clean: The world’s smallest dishwasher
item.description: Loch Electronics' latest innovation, the Capsule Solo, is a compact kitchen countertop dishwasher that can clean up to 35 dishes in just 15 minutes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: X requests it be reinstated in Brazil after complying with judge's orders, sources say
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — In the high-stakes showdown between the world’s richest man and a Brazilian Supreme Court justice, Elon Musk blinked. Musk’s social media site X has complied with Alexandre de Moraes’ orders and requested its service be reestablished in the country, two sources said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Police are probing apparent cyber vandalism on Wi-Fi networks at UK train stations
item.description: LONDON (AP) — U.K. transport officials and police said Thursday they are investigating a “cyber-security incident” after users of public Wi-Fi networks at the country’s biggest railway stations reported being shown anti-Muslim messages.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation
